rationale: Plan-forward decomposability approval still holds at 18d7ea3f. Removing the .golangci.yml carve-out and renumbering to R1-R9 does not disturb the natural 4-bead cut: B1 dead-code deletion, B2 setup managed-doc unification plus codex symlink test and chainBeads fold, B3 complete/phase perf pair, B4 guardrails restoration plus spec-init alias dedup. For the cleanest plan, the two R1 deletions that overlap later work should ride with their owning beads: setup.hasManagedBlock in B2, phase.FindActiveBeadForEpic in B3. That is a plan-shaping note, not a spec blocker, because requirements can be implemented across beads while preserving acceptance coverage. The ADR touchpoints are plan-gate viable: all cited ADRs are Accepted, each intersects at least one impacted domain, and together they cover workflow, core, execution, and context-system under internal/validate/plan.go checkADRCitations/checkADRCoverage.

findings.finding: Round-1 R6 approval remains valid after requirements were renumbered 1-9 and stale .golangci.yml carve-out removal was deferred to wave 2. The 4-bead decomposition still fits the validator's frontmatter, bead-section, and advisory decomposition-quality checks.

Plan author should assign the R1 deletion of setup.hasManagedBlock to the setup bead (B2), because it overlaps the same internal/setup managed-doc extraction surface. Keeping it in a broad dead-code bead would create avoidable same-path ordering pressure.

Plan author should assign the R1 deletion of phase.FindActiveBeadForEpic to the complete/phase perf bead (B3). This keeps internal/phase ownership together with the new phase.FetchChildren wrapper; unlike setup, it is mostly package-level tidiness rather than a hard textual conflict.

A plan citing ADR-0030, ADR-0033, ADR-0034, ADR-0035, ADR-0036, and ADR-0037 will satisfy checkADRCoverage for the spec's impacted domains: workflow is covered by 0034/0035/0036/0037, core by 0035, execution by 0030/0035/0037, and context-system by 0033. Each citation also intersects the impacted-domain set, so checkADRCitations should not flag irrelevance.
